title: Zaluzianskya minima
intro: Zaluzianskya minima, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial herb belonging to the evening primrose family (Oenotheraceae) that is native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of eczema and other skin conditions, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health by lowering cholesterol levels. Bioactive constituents of Zaluzianskya minima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Zaluzianskya minima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, which is now often merged into Plantaginaceae or Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales. The genus Zaluzianskya is a group of plants native to Southern Africa, and Zaluzianskya minima is one of the species within this genus. Zaluzianskya minima is a small, flowering plant that is often found in rocky or grassy areas. The exact classification of Zaluzianskya minima may vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed to be part of the family Plantaginaceae.
